title: TAS inputs
description: Design specification (the contract the converter must meet) plus operating points (specific test conditions within that contract). All values in SI units (V, A, W, °C, Hz, s). Fields here are REQUIREMENTS only — design choices like modulationType / controlMode are decisions made by the design pipeline, not inputs.

inputType.description: Source type. Selects whether input rectifier / PFC stages are needed.

inputVoltage.description: Allowed input-voltage range, V. For AC inputs this is RMS line voltage.

lineFrequency.description: AC line frequency, Hz. Required iff inputType is AC; forbidden for dc.

outputs.description: Output rails the converter must produce. Multi-output supplies list each rail.

switchingFrequency.description: Design-wide switching-frequency constraint, Hz. {nominal} = fixed-frequency design; {minimum, maximum} = allowed range (variable-frequency topologies, EMC band exclusion); omit entirely = pipeline picks freely. The actual fsw at each operating point is reported in outputs, not here.

isolationVoltage.description: Required galvanic isolation working voltage, V RMS, between input and outputs. Presence of this field implies isolation is required; omit if non-isolated is acceptable.

powerFactorMinimum.description: Minimum required input power factor (AC inputs only). Drives active-PFC stage decision. Forbidden for dc inputType.

bidirectional.description: If true, topology must support reverse power flow (drives synchronous rectification / 4-quadrant switch choices).

holdUpTimeMinimum.description: Minimum hold-up time after loss of AC input, s. Pushes architecture toward two-stage (PFC + DC-DC with bulk cap). AC inputs only; forbidden for dc.

name.description: Identifier unique within designRequirements.outputs[]. Used to join with operatingPoint.outputs[] and outputs.json results.

voltage.description: Nominal output voltage with tolerance, V. Use a negative nominal for negative rails (polarity is encoded in sign).

regulation.description: Regulation mode. 'both' covers CC/CV chargers; 'constantPower' a CP-limited envelope (EV fast-charge modules); 'fixedRatio' an open-loop K = Vout/Vin transformer-like output (bus converters, e.g. Vicor BCM); 'unregulated' a ratio-ish output with no closed loop (DOSA unregulated class).

ratio.description: Vout/Vin transformation ratio, V/V. Required for regulation 'fixedRatio'; optional for 'unregulated'; forbidden otherwise.

operatingPoint.description: A single test condition: scalar Vin, Tamb, and per-output load specification.

name.description: Identifier unique within this inputs document (e.g. 'full_load_Vin_min'). Used to join with outputs.operatingPoints[].

inputVoltage.description: Vin at this point, V (RMS for AC).

outputs.description: Per-output load condition. One entry per designRequirements.outputs[] rail (matched by name).

operatingPointOutput.description: Load condition for one output rail at one operating point: the converter's output-side boundary condition (the dual of inputVoltage), so it lives here per operating point — NOT a converter stage. Provide exactly one of current or power (the load magnitude); voltage is an optional setpoint override (programmable supplies, CC/CV charger phases); loadType selects the small-signal behaviour.

voltage.description: Output voltage setpoint at this OP, V. Omit to use the nominal from designRequirements.

current.description: Load current at this OP, A. Provide exactly one of current or power.

power.description: Load power at this OP, W. Provide exactly one of current or power.

loadType.description: Small-signal behaviour of the load at this operating point. 'resistive' = R = V^2/P (or V/I) — the conventional design/sim default; 'constantCurrent' = ideal current sink; 'constantPower' = constant-power load (CPL: negative incremental resistance dv/di<0, the classic downstream-regulated-converter instability case). Magnitude comes from current/power above. Omit → 'resistive'. Battery and downstream-converter loads need additional parameters (OCV+R_int / a converter reference) and will be added as a discriminated variant when required.
